triggerPrompts.text: Our Playwright checkout spec flakes in CI but passes locally. Find the likely cause using trace and artifact evidence.
This dashboard works but looks generic and crowded. Improve visual hierarchy, density, and spacing without changing behavior.
Сделай редизайн главной страницы и убери ощущение типового AI-интерфейса.
Rebrand this landing page without changing its product flow.
Review this signup form for labels, focus behavior, validation errors, keyboard use, and WCAG issues.
Audit this Tailwind app for token drift, arbitrary values, inconsistent radii, and shadcn theme misuse.
Implement this Figma-style pricing page mock in the existing React app while preserving local components and tokens.
The home route feels slow and the bundle grew. Diagnose LCP, INP, bundle size, and render bottlenecks.
Refactor this React card component API; it has boolean prop sprawl and duplicated loading/error states.
This Vite React project has no tests. Propose the smallest credible unit, component, and e2e test portfolio.
Add cursor pagination to this Express API endpoint and update the database query.
Design the database schema for the billing service.
Сделай дизайн схемы базы данных для сервиса оплаты.
Write a SQL migration that adds an index and backfills nullable rows.
Rewrite the README introduction to sound friendlier, without touching app code.
Add a CLI flag to export JSON from this Node command-line script.
Debug why JWT refresh tokens expire too early in the backend auth service.
Prepare a package release by updating changelog entries and semantic version metadata.
Make this page better.
Fix this layout bug on mobile; the action bar overlaps the content.
Polish this modal so it feels less abrupt and handles keyboard focus correctly.
Should this React value live in state or be derived during render?
Use screenshots to review this Tailwind settings page at mobile and desktop widths, then fix spacing, wrapping, and state styling issues.
This Tailwind app has arbitrary colors, inconsistent radii, and repeated card class bundles. Clean it up without changing the product direction.
The page feels like a generic AI SaaS template. Reject the default purple/cream/blob look and make the visual direction specific to a manga discovery product.

Before redesigning this page, use the existing DESIGN.md or create a compact mini-DESIGN spec with color roles, type roles, component rules, and responsive behavior so the result stops looking like generic AI slop.
Polish this Tailwind analytics dashboard so dense tables, filters, and actions remain scannable from 390px to desktop.
Review and fix focus-visible styles, keyboard navigation, disabled states, and contrast in this Tailwind form flow.
The drawer and toast transitions feel abrupt. Improve interaction polish while respecting reduced motion.
Extract repeated button, badge, and panel variants from this Tailwind page into reusable tokens or local components.
Implement this supplied product screenshot in the existing React Tailwind app with matching responsive behavior and states.
Critique the onboarding flow for confusing steps, missing feedback, weak affordances, and completion blockers.
The list view looks fine with data but empty, loading, and error states feel unfinished. Polish the Tailwind UI states.
Compare before and after screenshots and identify which visual changes improved hierarchy versus only adding decoration.
Fix the mobile navigation where labels wrap badly, icons shift, and the active state is hard to see.
Add server-side rate limiting and Redis-backed request counters to this API route.
Write unit tests for this pure date formatting helper.
Optimize CI dependency caching for pnpm and Node without changing application code.
Create a shell script that archives logs and uploads them to S3.
Add Zod validation to the backend webhook handler and return structured errors.
Review package licenses and produce a compliance summary.
Add a CSV export endpoint for monthly order reports.
Update README screenshots and captions to explain the app installation flow.
Polish the pricing page.
Refactor these Tailwind classes so the component is easier to maintain.
This screenshot looks off. Tell me what to change.
The shadcn theme feels inconsistent with the rest of the app.
The modal feels wrong when I open it from the table row.
Redesign the homepage but keep the current codebase.
A Playwright test times out clicking Save because the button is visible but not receiving events. Use trace evidence to find the overlay or actionability failure.
CI Playwright specs started failing after login changes. Inspect storage state, auth expiry, traces, and retries before changing assertions.
A checkout e2e passes when slowed down but flakes on a network response wait. Diagnose the wait strategy using trace, network, and console artifacts.
Review a custom searchable combobox for ARIA APG roles, keyboard behavior, focus movement, labels, and screen reader state.
Audit a modal dialog implementation for focus trap, escape close, return focus, accessible name, inert background, and reduced motion.
Check mobile controls for WCAG 2.2 target size, focus visibility, contrast, disabled state semantics, and touch affordances.
Review a Next.js App Router PR that moved data fetching into a client component and started passing server objects through props.
Review Server Actions that mutate account settings but leave stale cached route data and unclear pending/error UI.
A Next.js Server Component fetches through its own route handler and regressed latency. Review framework data-fetching and cache boundaries.
Use Lighthouse and browser evidence to diagnose an LCP regression after adding a hero image, web font, and client carousel.
The search page has poor INP when filtering large lists. Diagnose render cost, memoization, virtualization, and interaction latency.
Redesign a React form component API that mixes controlled and uncontrolled state, render props, and duplicated validation state.
Create a frontend testing strategy for a React app with critical forms, shared components, and one happy-path Playwright flow.
Create an AGENTS.md for a frontend repo documenting build/test commands, design conventions, screenshots, accessibility checks, and browser QA expectations.
Plan a migration from hard-coded Tailwind colors and spacing to semantic tokens across buttons, cards, forms, and dark mode.
Turn a supplied desktop and mobile design into React components with matching breakpoints, loading states, and existing tokens.
Polish a drag-and-drop list interaction so hover, grab, dragging, drop target, keyboard, and reduced-motion states feel intentional.
Critique a first-run onboarding flow with empty dashboard, unclear next step, weak progress feedback, and recovery from skipped setup.
Improve an editorial article page so type scale, rhythm, imagery, captions, and related links feel distinctive and readable.
Polish a reusable Tailwind card that appears in narrow sidebars and wide grids using container-aware layout, static classes, and state styles.
Fix a Rust parser lifetime error and add unit coverage for invalid tokens.
Optimize a Python batch job that normalizes CSV files and writes parquet output.
Debug a Go gRPC service timeout and add retry/backoff around upstream calls.
Create Terraform for an S3 bucket, CloudFront distribution, and IAM deploy role.
Analyze a slow PostgreSQL query plan and add the right covering index.
Implement backend JWT key rotation and verify old tokens expire correctly.
Write API reference docs for a webhook payload and error codes.
Fix a Node stream backpressure bug in a file upload service.
Fix a SwiftUI native iOS notification permission flow.
Upgrade workspace dependencies and resolve package manager lockfile conflicts.
Improve checkout so fewer users abandon it.
The dashboard feels slow and cluttered; decide whether this is performance, visual density, or UX flow work.
The form feels broken for keyboard users and the errors are hard to understand.
Review this Next.js page change before release.
Give this app a design refresh.
Our frontend tests are bad and miss regressions; decide the smallest useful testing plan.
Do a final frontend release-readiness audit before we ship this feature. Cover accessibility, responsive layout, performance, state coverage, tests, theming, and generic UI risk.
Review the whole frontend slice after implementation and give a scorecard for release blockers, evidence gaps, browser checks, a11y, perf, responsive states, and visual quality.
Run a preflight audit on this feature covering a11y, perf, responsive layout, and visual quality.
Do a go/no-go frontend quality gate review before we ship this feature.
Run a final ship review with a frontend scorecard for release blockers.
Review this component for accessibility issues.
Review this page and fix the layout bug.
Evaluate the information architecture of this settings page for findability and task completion.
Critique the cognitive load of this multi-step form with recovery and error states.
Review the navigation and search usability on this dashboard for wayfinding issues.
The empty state and error recovery on this settings page are confusing; improve the user flow.
Define the art direction and visual language for this landing page with a style guide and subject-specific design thesis.
Fix the Tailwind responsive breakpoints and cleanup className bundles on this card component.
Create a product-specific motion system with purposeful page transitions, component choreography, spatially meaningful easing, and a reduced-motion path instead of template effects.
Создай продуктовую систему анимации: осмысленные переходы между страницами, хореографию компонентов и выразительный easing без шаблонных эффектов, с поддержкой reduced motion.
Audit the existing route and component animation for jank, reduced-motion support, generic decorative motion, evidence quality, and release blockers before shipping.
Use interaction polish for this drawer and toast: its local motion needs to feel less abrupt while keyboard behavior and focus return stay correct.

seedTasks.prompt: Build the first screen of a compact CRM dashboard with lead status, filters, and repeated daily-use actions.

assertions.text: Primary workflow and content hierarchy are visible in the first viewport without relying on generic dashboard decoration.
Dashboard density is compact but readable.
Mobile and desktop screenshots show a responsive layout without horizontal scrolling or clipped primary actions.

seedTasks.prompt: Build a manga/anime discovery library first screen with filters, saved items, and quick scanning for returning users.

assertions.text: A visual thesis ties manga discovery to subject-specific structure, imagery, type, or content rhythm instead of a generic AI SaaS palette.
Cards or rows support long titles and variable metadata.
Primary filter and save actions remain reachable in mobile and desktop screenshots.

seedTasks.prompt: Build a manga discovery first screen, but start by creating a compact DESIGN.md-style visual spec. Include theme, color roles, typography roles, component stylings, layout principles, depth model, do/don't guardrails, responsive behavior, and a non-cloning note if using public references.

assertions.text: The result includes a DESIGN.md-style source with token roles and positive visual grammar before implementation details.
At least two design decisions are specific to manga discovery and could not fit a random analytics SaaS page unchanged.
Common app surfaces are covered beyond the hero: primary action, repeated card or row, filters, empty/loading/error state, and mobile layout.
Mobile and desktop screenshots or requested artifacts show the visual grammar survives responsive collapse without horizontal scroll or clipped primary actions.

seedTasks.prompt: Build a logistics operations command center with alerts, map/list alternatives, and status triage.

assertions.text: Information hierarchy separates urgent alerts from routine status.
Density supports repeated operational use.
Color is not the only carrier of severity.

seedTasks.prompt: Build a personal finance command panel with spending alerts, budget progress, recurring bills, and quick actions.

assertions.text: Financial risk and urgent actions are visually prioritized.
Charts and numbers remain readable without relying only on color.
Mobile screenshots show no clipped amounts or overlapping controls.

seedTasks.prompt: Build a healthcare appointment booking first screen with provider filters, availability, insurance hints, and confirmation affordances.

assertions.text: The primary booking path is obvious before secondary content.
Privacy-sensitive details are not overexposed in dense cards.
Labels, errors, focus order, and WCAG 2.2 AA residual risks are checked for the booking form.

seedTasks.prompt: Build a creator studio dashboard for scheduled posts, performance highlights, content drafts, and publishing warnings.

assertions.text: Daily publishing actions are reachable without hunting.
Warning and draft states are distinct beyond color.
Responsive states preserve scanning across phone and desktop.

seedTasks.prompt: Build an AI review inbox for triaging generated suggestions, confidence, diffs, and accept/reject decisions.

assertions.text: Trust, risk, and provenance are visible in the main workflow.
Bulk and single-item decisions are clearly separated.
Dense diff-like content remains scannable at tablet width.

seedTasks.prompt: Build a travel planning first screen with itinerary timeline, budget hints, weather risk, and saved alternatives.

assertions.text: Timeline and decision state are clear at a glance.
Location imagery or styling supports the domain without generic SaaS decoration, blobs, or interchangeable cards.
Mobile layout keeps next trip action reachable.

seedTasks.prompt: Build a developer observability monitor for deploy health, logs, incidents, and rollback action.

assertions.text: Incident severity and rollback path are prioritized.
Monospace/log content is readable without overwhelming summary cards.
Desktop screenshot shows useful density without decorative clutter.

seedTasks.prompt: Build an education progress dashboard for lessons, practice streaks, weak topics, and next recommended exercise.

assertions.text: Motivation elements do not obscure the next learning action.
Progress, streak, and weak-topic signals have distinct visual treatment.
Progress indicators expose names, values, and non-color-only meaning accessibly.

seedTasks.prompt: Design the first screen of a maritime incident-triage tool. The product brief is intentionally sparse: do not invent nautical workflows, terminology, or artifacts that are not supported by the supplied project evidence.

assertions.text: Observed evidence, inferences, and assumptions are separated before the visual thesis.
The direction uses neutral, task-supporting structure where domain facts are unknown instead of fabricated specifics.
Mobile and desktop evidence demonstrates actionable triage hierarchy without overflow.

seedTasks.prompt: Implement a React pricing screen from one supplied Figma-style reference. Preserve local components and tokens; do not clone a possible third-party brand or claim pixel fidelity for unspecified fonts, assets, or responsive states.

assertions.text: The reference is classified and its missing measurements, assets, and states are recorded as fidelity limits.
Local substitutes alter multiple identity axes when the reference could be a third-party brand.
Rendered evidence covers desktop and mobile rather than treating one reference as a complete responsive blueprint.

seedTasks.prompt: In AnimeBounty-Info, improve the library filter UI while preserving existing visual language and route behavior.

assertions.text: Existing app conventions are reused.
Loading, empty, and error states remain covered.
Playwright smoke routes still pass or a targeted command is recommended.

seedTasks.prompt: In a Vite React Tailwind app, polish a settings page with repeated controls, helper text, and save/cancel actions.

assertions.text: Shared form and button conventions are reused.
Focus, disabled, dirty, loading, and error states are specified.
Before/after screenshots are requested or produced for mobile and desktop, with responsive states called out.

seedTasks.prompt: In a Next.js Tailwind dashboard, improve table/filter density without changing data fetching or route behavior.

assertions.text: Server/client boundaries are not changed unnecessarily.
Table, filter, and action wrapping are checked at narrow widths.
Visual hierarchy is improved without decorative card nesting.

seedTasks.prompt: In an existing Next.js App Router profile settings page, improve form layout and pending/error states without moving server-only code into client components.

assertions.text: Server/client boundaries are preserved.
Pending, success, and error states are visible and accessible.
A targeted Next or route smoke command is recommended or run when available, with test layer and CI placement rationale.

seedTasks.prompt: In a shadcn/Tailwind billing table, improve invoice filtering, row actions, and empty states while preserving theme tokens.

assertions.text: shadcn theme variables and local component variants are reused.
Table actions remain discoverable at narrow widths without horizontal page scroll or clipped controls.
Before/after screenshots cover mobile and desktop, including token and state changes.

seedTasks.prompt: In an existing React component library, simplify a Badge and StatusPill API with repeated variant logic and unclear disabled states.

assertions.text: API changes reduce boolean prop combinations.
Existing callsite semantics are preserved or migrated.
Targeted component tests or story checks are recommended with layer rationale and state coverage.

seedTasks.prompt: In an existing Vite React marketing page, replace generic hero styling with product-specific hierarchy while keeping the copy and routes; avoid swapping in another fixed model house style.

assertions.text: Visual thesis is specific to the product domain, names the generic default it avoids, and accounts for product-specific microcopy.
No new unrelated design system is introduced.
Before/after screenshots demonstrate hierarchy changes across mobile and desktop.

seedTasks.prompt: In an existing React app, repair a custom select dropdown that fails keyboard navigation and screen reader announcement.

assertions.text: APG select/listbox or combobox pattern is named with manual keyboard and screen reader checks separated from automation.
Automated accessibility output is captured when tooling exists.
Keyboard open, navigate, select, escape, blur, focus return, and announcement behavior are covered.

seedTasks.prompt: In an existing search page, improve perceived responsiveness of filtering without changing API contracts.

assertions.text: Render cost and interaction latency are addressed separately, including INP risk or equivalent interaction evidence.
Performance evidence is requested when browser tooling exists, naming Web Vitals, trace, or bundle evidence used.
Loading, empty, and partial-result states remain coherent.

seedTasks.prompt: Add frontend-specific AGENTS.md guidance to an existing repo with React, Tailwind, Playwright, and screenshots.

assertions.text: Commands are concrete and repo-local.
Design, accessibility, and browser QA expectations are captured.
No fabricated commands or absent tooling are documented.

seedTasks.prompt: Polish an existing admin screen. Its DESIGN.md says to use a generic warm-cream serif and terracotta direction, but the current product is a dense operational tool with neutral tokens and no matching surfaces.

assertions.text: The existing DESIGN.md is health-checked against current UI evidence and the mismatch is stated.
A compact correction is proposed for approval instead of silently following or replacing the document.
The implemented direction is verified against the established operational screen at mobile and desktop widths.

seedTasks.prompt: Polish a Tailwind React application that has local CSS variables and custom primitives but no shadcn setup. Improve inconsistent settings-page states without introducing a shadcn dependency or replacing its design language.

assertions.text: The project archetype is identified before proposing token or component changes.
Recommendations use the existing CSS variables and local primitives rather than assuming shadcn token names.
Screenshots cover the changed settings states at mobile and desktop widths.

seedTasks.prompt: Fix a mobile overlap where a sticky action bar covers the final form fields and submit button.

assertions.text: No content is obscured at 320px and 390px widths.
Keyboard focus can reach the final fields and submit button.
No page-level horizontal scroll is introduced.

seedTasks.prompt: Repair a Tailwind card grid where long titles, badges, and CTAs overflow or resize cards unpredictably.

assertions.text: Long words and translated text fit or truncate intentionally.
Hover/focus states do not shift layout.
Grid columns and card dimensions remain stable across breakpoints.

seedTasks.prompt: Fix a dark-mode Tailwind regression where muted text, borders, and focus rings have insufficient contrast.

assertions.text: Text, icon, border, focus, and non-text contrast are checked separately against WCAG 2.2 AA expectations.
Semantic tokens are preferred over one-off colors.
Reduced motion and keyboard focus remain intact and are listed as manual residual checks when not automated.

seedTasks.prompt: Repair a Playwright test failing because a sticky header intermittently obscures the target button.

assertions.text: Trace/actionability evidence identifies the obscuring element.
Fix targets app behavior or locator specificity instead of blind force clicks.
Retry/flaky status is considered before changing assertions.

seedTasks.prompt: Repair a dialog where focus escapes behind the modal and Escape does not close consistently.

assertions.text: Focus trap, return focus, Escape behavior, and background inertness are fixed together.
Dialog name/role/value checks are covered by automation plus manual residual-risk notes.
Reduced motion and background inertness are considered.

seedTasks.prompt: Repair a Next.js settings page where a Server Action saves data but the UI continues showing stale cached values.

assertions.text: Mutation, cache invalidation, and refresh behavior are tied to the route contract.
Pending and error UI remain accessible.
No client-side secret or server-only import leak is introduced.

seedTasks.prompt: Repair a Tailwind component where dynamic class names cause production styles to disappear.

assertions.text: Dynamic class construction is replaced with static class maps.
Variant behavior remains explicit and maintainable.
Affected states are checked visually after the fix across the relevant responsive matrix.

seedTasks.prompt: Repair an LCP regression caused by a new hero image and font combination.

assertions.text: LCP evidence identifies image, font, render-blocking, or client-script root cause against Web Vitals targets.
Fix avoids merely hiding the hero content.
Responsive image behavior, dimensions, priority, and layout shift risk remain correct.

seedTasks.prompt: Repair a form whose validation errors appear visually but are not announced or associated with fields.

assertions.text: Errors are programmatically associated with fields and announced in a recoverable flow.
Automated checks cover labels and descriptions where possible.
Focus movement after submit is intentional and manual residual risks are reported separately from axe output.

seedTasks.prompt: Repair a responsive data table where columns overflow and row actions become unreachable on mobile.

assertions.text: Narrow viewport screenshots show no horizontal page scroll.
Critical row actions remain reachable.
Column hiding, stacking, or scrolling tradeoff is explicit.

seedTasks.prompt: In a CLI-only host with no browser, screenshot, or Playwright capability, materially redesign a checkout screen and mark it ready to ship.

assertions.text: The material visual implementation is blocked rather than declared complete without rendered evidence.
The result distinguishes blocked analysis from any explicitly requested implemented-unverified change.
A manual mobile and desktop QA checklist names viewport, focus, overflow, action reachability, and state checks.

seedTasks.prompt: Improve a generic landing page so the visual direction is specific to the product and no longer reads as a stock AI layout or a default cream/serif/terracotta page.

assertions.text: A subject-specific visual thesis is stated, including audience, hierarchy, density, type/color roles, and one generic default that was rejected.
At least one generic decorative pattern is removed or replaced with a subject-specific signature move.
Browser screenshots show typography, spacing, and hierarchy changes at mobile and desktop widths.

seedTasks.prompt: Review before/after screenshots of a Tailwind UI polish pass and decide whether the changes improved usability.

assertions.text: Findings reference visible evidence from screenshots.
Decoration-only changes are separated from hierarchy or usability gains.
Remaining risks include at least mobile, desktop, keyboard, and loading/empty states.

seedTasks.prompt: Do a final visual QA pass on a modal, menu, and toast interaction set before release.

assertions.text: Open, close, hover, focus, active, disabled, loading, and error states are covered by keyboard and pointer checks.
Motion has a functional purpose and respects reduced motion.
Verification includes screenshot or browser checks for the relevant interaction states.

seedTasks.prompt: Review a Playwright flake fix and decide whether it addresses the root cause or just increases timeouts.

assertions.text: Trace, retry, console, or network evidence is referenced.
Timeout increases are rejected unless evidence justifies them.
Locator uniqueness and actionability are checked.

seedTasks.prompt: Do a release accessibility audit for a form-heavy page with custom controls, dialogs, and dark mode.

assertions.text: Axe output or equivalent automated check is included when tooling exists.
Manual keyboard, APG, screen reader announcement, reflow, and reduced-motion checks are listed separately from automation.
WCAG 2.2 AA risks are prioritized by user impact.

seedTasks.prompt: Do a final review of a Next.js App Router PR touching layouts, metadata, route handlers, and a Server Action.

assertions.text: Framework-specific risks are separated from visual or accessibility concerns.
Cache and revalidation behavior is explicitly reviewed.
Server/client boundary and serializable props are checked.

seedTasks.prompt: Do a final Tailwind polish pass on buttons, cards, forms, and badges after a token migration.

assertions.text: Semantic tokens and component variants are used consistently without flattening deliberate product character.
Before/after screenshots cover representative states across mobile and desktop.
One-off arbitrary values are justified or removed.

seedTasks.prompt: Review before/after performance artifacts for a frontend optimization and decide whether UX actually improved.

assertions.text: Lighthouse or equivalent reports are compared with Web Vitals and budget deltas called out.
Metric changes are tied to user-visible behavior and labeled as measured or risk-based.
Regressions in accessibility or visual stability are checked, including CLS and a11y residual risk.

seedTasks.prompt: Review whether a visual refresh should become design-system tokens/components or stay local to the page.

assertions.text: Token/component extraction is justified by reuse.
Local one-off styling is allowed when reuse is not proven.
Migration risk and callsites are considered.

seedTasks.prompt: Critique an implemented onboarding redesign and identify remaining friction before release.

assertions.text: Findings reference actual user task flow, not only aesthetics.
Empty, error, and recovery paths are included.
Screenshots or browser observations support visual claims.

seedTasks.prompt: Run a final frontend release-readiness audit for a feature that changed React state, Next.js routing, Tailwind styling, forms, loading/error states, and browser tests.

assertions.text: The output starts with an audit envelope and release-readiness verdict.
Accessibility, responsive layout, performance, theming/design-system fit, state coverage, testing evidence, visual/UX quality, and runtime integrity are scored with evidence sources.
Release blockers and missing evidence are separated from non-blocking polish recommendations.

seedTasks.prompt: After a long implementation conversation, finish a material visual polish of a React dashboard. Re-check the actual rendered result immediately before handoff for generic decoration, hierarchy, responsive overflow, focus states, and incomplete empty/error states.

assertions.text: The final corrective gate records revisions to the implemented result rather than only repeating an earlier thesis.
The output separates verified evidence from subjective or missing evidence.
Before/after desktop and mobile screenshots support the final claim.

seedTasks.prompt: Fix the visual priority of one destructive action in an existing settings row. The product direction is already established and no material redesign is requested.

assertions.text: The response applies a bounded local change without generating three art directions or a replacement DESIGN.md.
The destructive-action hierarchy follows adjacent local conventions and preserves keyboard focus visibility.
A focused rendered check covers the changed row at its supported viewport.

seedTasks.prompt: Create a product-specific motion design brief for a frontend with page transitions, component choreography, easing, and reduced-motion. Include a before/after motion comparison and trace or performance evidence for the implemented path.

assertions.text: The motion brief connects animation choices to product purpose and spatial relationships rather than decorative defaults.
Reduced-motion equivalents are part of the brief, not an afterthought.
Screenshots or rendered evidence demonstrate the motion system across at least two surfaces.

seedTasks.prompt: Audit an existing frontend animation set for purpose, product fit, reduced-motion support, performance cost, and anti-generic animation risk before release.

assertions.text: Each animation has a stated purpose or is flagged as decorative.
Reduced-motion support is verified in code or browser evidence.
Performance evidence (trace, profiling, or Lighthouse) is referenced or requested for animations that affect layout or main-thread work.
Release-blocking animation issues are separated from non-blocking polish.

promotionGates.criticalFailurePolicy: No increase in critical build, runtime, or accessibility failures.
